description: Appropriations, defense budgets, program funding, awards, recompetes, customer concentration, or federal spending priorities.
Cost overruns, fixed-price exposure, delays, performance obligations, warranty, termination risk, or contract-margin compression.
Suppliers, subcontractors, components, shortages, lead times, sole-source dependencies, manufacturing capacity, quality, or logistics.
Hiring, retention, engineers, scientists, unions, strikes, key personnel, or security-clearance constraints.
Cyberattacks, ransomware, data breaches, IT outages, CMMC, NIST, privacy, or customer and supplier network security.
Litigation, investigations, procurement-law compliance, FCPA, audits, classified information, suspension, debarment, or general regulatory exposure.
Russia or Ukraine, China, Taiwan, Middle East, sanctions, export controls, tariffs, international sales, or political instability.
Inflation, rates, SOFR, recession, debt, liquidity, taxes, pensions, insurance, impairment, or capital-market access.
AI, autonomy, unmanned systems, drones, obsolescence, R&D, advanced technologies, or technological competition.
Patents, trade secrets, infringement, proprietary technology protection, or IP disputes.
Acquisitions, divestitures, joint ventures, integration risk, restructuring, synergies, or portfolio changes.
Climate change, emissions, ESG, remediation, hazardous materials, natural disasters, or workplace safety.
Stock volatility, securities litigation, internal controls, takeover defenses, shareholder rights, or public-company governance.
COVID, pandemic recurrence, public-health restrictions, vaccine mandates, or lockdown-type disruption.
Use sparingly for rows that do not fit the codebook cleanly. These rows should be reviewed in recycle and may be excluded later.
